headline: Oil Above $100 Casts a Long Shadow Over Friday's Open
body_html:
Futures point to a cautious Friday open after Thursday's broad retreat, with crude oil's surge past $100 a barrel reshaping the risk calculus across asset classes overnight. Asian equities sank and sovereign bonds struggled as traders priced in the inflationary bite of elevated energy costs, while Treasury yields pulled back slightly from levels last seen near January 2025 highs — offering only modest relief to rate-sensitive sectors.

The geopolitical backdrop remains the dominant driver. Ongoing U.S. military strikes against Iran — now in their thirteenth consecutive night — and intensifying Houthi activity in the Red Sea have tightened shipping corridors, with tankers diverting around the Strait of Hormuz at a reported cost premium of $2.5 million and a full month of additional transit time. Ship traffic through the strait has held steady at three transits for three consecutive days, a figure traders are watching closely for any further deterioration.

On the domestic calendar, watch for any fresh commentary on energy supply chains and consumer prices. Thursday's session ended with 302 names lower against 196 gainers in our 501-stock universe, with Software, Consumer, and Homebuilders bearing the steepest sector losses. Healthcare was the sole sector to finish in the green, up 0.61%.

briefs.headline: Crude Tops $100 as Red Sea Attacks Escalate
briefs.body: Oil settled above $100 a barrel Thursday as Houthi strikes on Red Sea shipping lanes intensified, putting the commodity on track for a weekly gain. The price milestone is amplifying inflation concerns globally, pressuring bonds and weighing on equities sensitive to input costs and consumer spending.

briefs.headline: Treasury Market Steadies But Yields Remain Elevated
briefs.body: Ten-year Treasury yields edged back from recent peaks Thursday but remain near levels not seen since early 2025, keeping pressure on growth-oriented sectors. The bond market's struggle to find footing reflects the market's difficulty reconciling persistent energy-driven inflation risk with any near-term rate relief.

briefs.headline: Shipping Detours Add Months and Millions to Oil Transport
briefs.body: The mathematics of Middle East avoidance are becoming clearer: rerouting tankers away from the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb is adding roughly a month to transit times and approximately $2.5 million in costs per voyage. The burden is rippling through energy supply chains and airline fuel procurement alike, with at least one U.S. carrier resorting to unconventional domestic fuel logistics.

briefs.headline: Congress Divided on Constraining Iran War Powers
briefs.body: The U.S. House passed a measure aimed at limiting presidential authority over military action against Iran, but the Senate moved in the opposite direction, backing the administration's stance. The legislative split leaves the geopolitical trajectory uncertain and keeps a risk premium firmly embedded in oil and defense-adjacent equities heading into the weekend.

briefs.headline: OpenAI Breach Sparks Legislative Push for AI Emergency Controls
briefs.body: A security incident involving OpenAI and Hugging Face has prompted lawmakers to advance a bill that would establish a formal 'AI Kill Switch' mechanism. The episode adds a new regulatory overhang to the AI infrastructure trade and arrives as scrutiny of the sector's cash consumption is already intensifying among institutional investors.

briefs.headline: Thursday's Bright Spots: Life Sciences and Industrial Security Stand Out
briefs.body: Against a broadly negative tape, Thermo Fisher climbed 8.71% and Medpace surged 14.71%, helping Healthcare finish as Thursday's only positive sector. Allegion added 10.45% in the Industrials-Security space, while Gentherm's 26.52% jump — the session's largest gain — underscored how earnings catalysts can cut sharply through an otherwise defensive market.
